description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a cylindrical stone shaft. The well is topped with a pitched wooden roof covered in gray, shingle-like tiles, one of which is visibly broken or missing. Two sturdy wooden posts support the roof, and a horizontal wooden beam spans the top, holding a crank mechanism for drawing water. The stonework at the base and shaft is textured and irregular, giving a rustic appearance. The well is open at the top, with a visible winding handle and rope mechanism for lowering and raising a bucket.
description: A rustic stone water well with a pitched wooden roof, shingle tiles, and a crank mechanism for drawing water.
description_view_0: Front view showing the well's circular stone base, open shaft, wooden posts, and the crank handle on the left side.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the triangular wooden roof structure and the stone base, with the crank mechanism partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the shingled roof and the stone base, with no crank handle visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the wooden roof structure and stone base, with the crank mechanism on the right.
attribute_well_cover_design: No full cover; open shaft with pitched wooden roof and shingle tiles, one tile broken.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: Visible crank handle and rope mechanism for drawing water, likely metal and wood.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall around the shaft, approximately waist-high, with a wooden roof supported by posts.
